T3.justification: DB layer wraps oracledb.DatabaseError into SQLExecutionError (db.py execute); ToolExecutor catches errors and returns JSON error payloads; __main__.py wraps run_question in try/except BLE001 with friendly stderr message. Config validation raises RuntimeError with explanation. Minor gap: LLM API calls in llm.py have no try/except wrapping.

T4.justification: Type hints present on public functions/dataclasses across config.py, db.py, agent.py, tools.py, validation.py. Modules are well under 500 LoC, naming is clear, docstrings on classes and tools, no obvious dead code or duplication.

T7.justification: OracleClient._assert_safe_select whitelists SELECT/WITH only and rejects mutating keywords (tests in test_db_safety.py confirm). _normalize_sql strips markdown/semicolons. System prompt instructs DATE 'YYYY-MM-DD' literals. SQL is generated by LLM without parameterization, but only read-only and guarded by whitelist; values come from LLM, not direct user interpolation.

B1.justification: final_answer tool requires Russian text with numbers/units; system prompt mandates formatting like '1 234 567 890.12 ₽'; README example shows '345 802 507.40 ₽'. Result truncation flagged. agent.py separates final NL answer to stdout from diagnostics on stderr.

B2.justification: validation.py provides comprehensive sanity checks: min/max rows, non_null_columns, numeric_ranges, expected_columns, NULL scalar detection, truncation note. System prompt enforces validate_result before final_answer. test_validation.py covers each case.

B3.justification: Agent handles no-tool-call stagnation via no_tool_strikes forcing final_answer (agent.py); empty/NULL results flagged by validator; unknown table returns error in tools.py; system prompt instructs to default to last available month for ambiguous periods. Could clarify ambiguous NL more explicitly but degradation paths are present.

B5.justification: Config via env vars / .env (config.py) — no hardcoded creds. Diagnostic logging via stderr with --quiet flag. Context manager for connection lifecycle (db.py __enter__/__exit__). Exit codes distinguished (0/1/2). Missing: no Dockerfile, no CI workflow, no structured logging (uses print), no retry logic or connection pooling.
